---
title: "Selection Bias & Missing Data"
format:
  html: default
execute:
  echo: false
  eval: true
---

```{python}
#| label: pipeline
#| include: false

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from step1_prepare_image import prepare_image
from step2_create_stipple import create_stipple
from step4_create_block_letter import create_block_letter_s
from step5_create_masked import create_masked_stipple
from create_meme import create_statistics_meme

# Step 1: Load and prepare image
gray_image = prepare_image("Jacob.jpeg", max_size=512)

# Step 2: Blue-noise stippling
stipple_pattern, samples = create_stipple(
    gray_image,
    percentage=0.08,
    sigma=0.9,
    content_bias=0.9,
    noise_scale_factor=0.1,
    extreme_downweight=0.5,
    extreme_threshold_low=0.2,
    extreme_threshold_high=0.8,
    extreme_sigma=0.1,
)

# Step 4: Block letter "S" matching image dimensions
h, w = gray_image.shape
block_letter = create_block_letter_s(h, w, letter="S", font_size_ratio=0.9)

# Step 5: Apply mask — erase stipple dots inside the "S"
masked_stipple = create_masked_stipple(stipple_pattern, block_letter, threshold=0.5)

# Final: Assemble and save four-panel meme
create_statistics_meme(
    original_img=gray_image,
    stipple_img=stipple_pattern,
    block_letter_img=block_letter,
    masked_stipple_img=masked_stipple,
    output_path="my_statistics_meme.png",
    dpi=150,
    background_color="white",
)
```

```{python}
#| label: display-meme
#| fig-cap: "Four-panel statistics meme demonstrating selection bias"

from IPython.display import Image, display
display(Image("my_statistics_meme.png"))
```

The original image (Reality) represents the true population, while the stippled
version (Your Model) captures our data collection — dots concentrated in darker,
information-rich regions. When selection bias systematically removes data in the
shape of an "S", the resulting Estimate is no longer representative of reality,
illustrating how non-random missing data patterns lead to biased conclusions.
